--- title: "[CoxNetCVResult]{.doc-object-name .doc-class .doc-label .doc-label-class}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Result of cross-validated penalizer selection for `CoxNet`. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_penalized.py#L500-L650){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.CLASS} ```python CoxNetCVResult( penalizers, mean_scores, std_scores, n_nonzero, metric, l1_ratio, k ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Returned by `cv_coxnet()`. Stores the cross-validation scores at every tested penalizer value, identifies the best penalizer (highest concordance or lowest Brier score), and applies the *one-standard-error rule* for a more parsimonious alternative. Penalizers are ordered from *largest* (most regularized, sparsest model) to *smallest* (least regularized, densest model). ::: ## Attributes {.doc-attributes} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [penalizers_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Penalizer values tested, sorted descending. [mean_scores_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Mean cross-validation score at each penalizer. [std_scores_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Standard deviation of per-fold scores at each penalizer. [n_nonzero_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Average number of non-zero coefficients at each penalizer (averaged over cross-validation folds). [metric_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Metric used: `"concordance"` or `"brier"`. [l1_ratio_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Elastic-net mixing parameter fixed during the path search. [k_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Number of folds used. [best_penalizer_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Penalizer with the best mean cross-validation score. [best_score_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Mean score at `best_penalizer_`. [penalizer_1se_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Largest penalizer (most regularized) whose mean score is within one standard error of `best_score_`. Prefer this when parsimony matters. [score_1se_]{.doc-parameter-name} : Mean score at `penalizer_1se_`. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} Run cross-validated penalizer selection and inspect the result: ::: {#d5d650f4 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cols = ["age", "sex", "ph.ecog", "ph.karno", "wt.loss"] cv_result = gw.cv_coxnet(y, lung[cols], seed=23) cv_result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` CoxNetCV (concordance, ↑ higher is better, l1_ratio=1.0, 5-fold) best penalizer : 0.10767 (mean concordance: 0.6356) 1-SE penalizer : 0.16435 (mean concordance: 0.6205) 50 penalizers tested, range [0.000218, 0.218] ``` ::: ::: The best penalizer and the more conservative one-standard-error penalizer are available as attributes: ::: {#97733ea9 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} cv_result.best_penalizer_ ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` 0.10767101882396805 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#8583661c .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} cv_result.penalizer_1se_ ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` 0.16435097767696763 ``` ::: ::: ## Methods {.doc-methods}
Name Description
to_frame() Return the CV path as a tidy DataFrame, one row per penalizer.
### [to_frame()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-method} ::: {.doc-subject} Return the CV path as a tidy DataFrame, one row per penalizer. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_penalized.py#L613-L650){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python to_frame(*, format=None) ``` ::: #### Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [format]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Output format: `None` (default), `"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`. When `None`, a backend is auto-detected. ::: #### Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [DataFrame]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Columns: `penalizer`, `mean_score`, `std_score`, `n_nonzero`. Rows are ordered from largest to smallest penalizer. ::: #### Examples {.doc-examples} Export the full CV path as a Polars DataFrame for plotting or further analysis: ::: {#101605be .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cols = ["age", "sex", "ph.ecog", "ph.karno", "wt.loss"] result = gw.cv_coxnet(y, lung[cols], seed=23) result.to_frame(format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
